name: Center for Pan Asian Community Services, INC
description_short: CPACS is a non-profit community service organization in Atlanta providing free housing counseling, legal/immigration assistance, health services, and financial support to underserved populations across 25+ languages.
description_long:
Center For Pan Asian Community Services, Inc. (CPACS) is a non-profit organization headquartered at 3510 Shallowford Road in Atlanta, Georgia. Founded to serve pan-Asian and underserved communities, the organization has grown into a comprehensive social services provider addressing multiple dimensions of community wellbeing and economic stability.

CPACS offers a broad spectrum of free and low-cost services including HUD-approved housing counseling (pre-purchase, post-purchase, and foreclosure prevention), legal and immigration services (citizenship classes, green card renewal, work permits), community health programs (HIV testing, substance abuse prevention, domestic violence support), senior and social services (LIHEAP benefits, health insurance enrollment, senior housing), youth education programs, and translation services in 25+ languages. They also operate food pantries, mobile health screenings, and vaccination clinics. Their housing counseling and immigration legal services are particularly relevant to credit and financial stability.

What distinguishes CPACS is their deep multilingual capacity (25+ languages with on-site translation), free transportation support, integration of health and financial services, and focus on immigrant and pan-Asian communities who often face barriers to traditional financial resources. They combine emergency assistance (food, energy, health) with longer-term financial coaching and legal pathway support, recognizing that credit and financial health are interconnected with housing security and legal status.

As a non-profit community service organization, CPACS is best suited for individuals seeking free housing counseling, immigration legal assistance, or emergency community support rather than credit repair or loan products. Their value proposition is comprehensive community integration rather than credit-specific solutions. They appear to be a legitimate, mission-driven organization, though their website does not highlight specific certifications (HUD-approval status, NFCC membership) that would strengthen their credibility as a financial counseling provider.
pros: Free HUD-approved housing counseling including foreclosure prevention and homebuyer workshops
Comprehensive legal and immigration services (citizenship, green card, work permits) with on-site interpretation
Services available in 25+ languages with free on-site translation
Free or low-cost emergency assistance (food pantry, LIHEAP energy assistance, rental assistance)
Integrated health services (HIV testing, substance abuse prevention, domestic violence support) that impact financial stability
Free transportation provided in many cases, removing access barriers
Serves vulnerable populations (immigrants, seniors, low-income families) who lack traditional financial service access
cons: Website does not explicitly state HUD-approval status or NFCC certification for housing/credit counseling, limiting verification of counselor credentials
Limited information about specific outcomes or effectiveness metrics for housing counseling or financial stability programs
Appears to focus on emergency/community support rather than in-depth credit repair or debt management counseling
No mention of credit monitoring, credit building tools, or credit score tracking services
Geographic limitation to Atlanta/DeKalb/Gwinnett area; services not available nationally
best_for: Immigrants and non-English speakers seeking legal pathway services and financial integration support
Low-income homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking pre-purchase counseling without cost barriers
Individuals needing emergency assistance (food, utility bills, rental help) combined with longer-term community support
Seniors and families seeking benefits enrollment, housing assistance, or health services with language accommodation
diagnosis: CPACS is best for immigrants, seniors, and low-income families in the Atlanta area seeking free community services that bundle housing counseling, legal assistance, health support, and emergency aid. The main caveat is that they are a comprehensive community service provider rather than a credit-focused organization, so consumers specifically seeking credit repair, debt consolidation, or credit building products should look elsewhere.
services: Green Card renewal and work permit assistance
Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) benefits enrollment
Senior services and senior housing assistance
Domestic violence prevention and support services
After-school and summer youth education programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
